sentences.text: Sam has a cat.
The cat is Pam.
Pam is a fat cat.
Pam can hop.
Pam can sit.
Pam can nap.
Sam can pet Pam.
Pam is fun.
Pam is on the mat.
The mat is hot.
Pam can not nap.
Pam can hop up.
Pam is on the cot.
Sam can pat Pam.
Pam is fun.
Sam is a man.

passages.imageAlt: A dusk scene over a small garden of flowers.

sentences.text: Tim has a hat.
The hat is fun.
The hat is on Tim.
Tim can sit on the cot.
The cot is in the sun.
The sun is hot.
Tim is hot.
The hat is on the mat.
The mat is in the hut.
Tim can not nap.
The pup is in the hut.
Tim can pet the pup.
The pup is fun.
Tim is a fan.

passages.imageAlt: A morning scene over a warm sunlit window over a sill.

sentences.text: Pat has a pup.
The pup can hop.
The pup is in a pit.
Pat can not pat the pup.
Pat is a man.
The pup can hop up.
Pat sat on the mat.
Pat can pat the pup.
I am Pat.
Pat is fun.
Pat can hum to the pup.
The pup can nap.
A pup is on the cot.
Pat is on the cot.
A pup is in the sun.

passages.imageAlt: A late afternoon scene over a small garden of flowers.

sentences.text: Sam has a hen.
The hen is in a pen.
A hen is fat.
The hen can not hop.
Sam can pet the hen.
Sam is fun.
Pam has a hen.
Pam can pet the hen.
The pen is on the mat.
The mat is hot.
A hen is hot.
Sam can fan the hen.
The fan is fun.
The hen can nap.
The hen is in the pen.

passages.imageAlt: A night scene over a small garden of flowers.

sentences.text: I am Sam.
I has a cat.
Sam has a cat.
The cat is Tim.
Tim is in a cup.
The cup is on the mat.
Sam can not pet Tim.
Tim is in the cup.
Sam is sad.
Sam is not fun.
Tim can hop up.
Tim is on the mat.
Sam can pet Tim.
Sam is fun.
Tim is fun.
Tim can nap on the mat.

passages.imageAlt: A dusk scene over a warm sunlit window over a sill.

sentences.text: Mom has a pot.
The pot is hot.
A pot is on the cot.
Mom can not sit on the cot.
The cot is hot.
Mom is in the hut.
The hut is fun.
Mom can sip.
Mom can hum.
A pot is on the mat.
Sam can pat the pot.
Sam is hot.
Sam can not pat the pot.
Mom can fan the pot.
The pot is on the mat.

passages.imageAlt: A morning scene over a warm sunlit window over a sill.

sentences.text: A frog sat on a log.
He sang a song with a duck.
The duck went for a swim.
Splash! The duck went under.
The frog gave a long jump.
He had to catch a fish.
Two ducks went past with chips.
The frog said thanks for the chips.
He sat with the ducks on a rock.
Then they sang a song with the wind.
The pond was a fun spot.
Frogs and ducks like ponds.
They went home when the sun went down.
The frog said he would come back.

passages.imageAlt: A dawn scene over a still pond with reeds.

sentences.text: A truck rumbled past my house.
It had a big lump of bricks.
The bricks went thump in the truck.
I went to look at the truck.
The truck stopped with a sharp bump.
A man jumped out with a wrench.
He had a bath of black grease.
The bricks slipped and they had cracks.
I sat on a bench and went munch munch.
I had crisps and a punch of milk.
When the truck went on, it sang a hum.
I waved as it went past.

passages.imageAlt: A midday scene over a country road winding through hills.

sentences.text: Mom got me a fish tank.
It had a pump and some plants.
I went to pick a fish for the tank.
I picked a small fish with a black tail.
The fish was quick like a flash.
I dropped the fish in with a splash.
The fish went up and down in the tank.
It went past the plants and the rocks.
Mom said the fish was a champ.
I went to bed with a big grin.
When I woke up, the fish was still quick.
The fish would be my friend.

passages.imageAlt: A late afternoon scene over a still pond with reeds.

sentences.text: Sam went to the shop with his dad.
They had a list and a cart.
Sam had to find some bread and milk.
He went past the chip stand and the fish stand.
He picked up a stack of buns.
He picked up some big shrimp.
His dad got him a snack.
Sam went munch munch as they went.
When they got home, Mom said thanks.
She set the bread and milk on the shelf.
Sam helped his dad with the bags.
Then they had lunch.

passages.imageAlt: A midday scene over a warm sunlit window over a sill.

sentences.text: Six pups ran in the back yard.
They went to dig in the dirt patch.
One pup had a red ball.
He went thump on the ball.
Then a small pup grabbed the ball.
The other pups did a chase.
They went round and round the yard.
The small pup hid by the fence.
He said in pup talk, You can not catch me.
When the sun set, the pups went in.
They had a long drink and a quick lunch.
Then they went to bed.

passages.imageAlt: A dusk scene over a quiet playground in a field.

sentences.text: Pat and Sam sat on a long bench.
They had lunch in a big sack.
Pat had a chunk of ham and bread.
Sam had a bunch of chips and a cup.
The wind sent a chill past the bench.
Pat said the chill was a big shock.
Sam went munch on a fresh chip.
Then they went for a quick stroll.
They went past a shop with red bricks.
They went past a man with a cap.
When they got back, the bench was wet.
It had a fresh dash of rain.

passages.imageAlt: A morning scene over a warm sunlit window over a sill.

sentences.text: Mom and I made a cake.
We had a big tin and a long pan.
We mixed eggs, milk, and a lump of cake mix.
The mix was thick and white.
I helped to blend it.
Mom put the pan in the hot stove.
I sat and sat.
The cake smelled like home.
When the time was up, mom took the cake from the stove.
It was tan on top.
We let it rest.
Then we put soft frosting on top.
I had a bit on my lips.
I hugged mom and said thanks.
The cake was the best.
I want to bake one again.

passages.imageAlt: A morning scene over a warm sunlit window over a sill.

sentences.text: Mike got a slick red bike.
He went to ride it on the lane.
His best pal Jane rode at his side.
They went past the pond and the big rock.
The wind was brisk.
Mike hopped off to fix the bike.
Jane helped him.
They got it fixed and rode on.
They rode up the tall hill.
Mike got up fast.
At the top, they ate snacks.
Jane gave Mike a slice of plum.
They sat in the shade.
Mike said this was the best ride.
Then they went home with big smiles on their lips.
It was fun.

passages.imageAlt: A midday scene over a country road winding through hills.

sentences.text: Pat got home from school.
She saw a small white note on the rug.
The note had a name on it.
It was for her dad.
The note said: Mr. Pace, come fix my sink.
Pat ran to give the note to dad.
Dad smiled and said thanks.
He had been hoping for a job.
Dad got his kit and went to fix the sink.
He hopped in the truck.
Pat hopped in too.
They got to the home and dad fixed the sink.
The wife was glad.
She gave Pat a slice of cake.
Pat liked the cake.

passages.imageAlt: A night scene over a forest path between trees.

sentences.text: Jane had a red kite.
She ran to lift it up.
The wind was strong and brisk.
She had a long string in her hand.
Jane went fast and let the kite go up.
The kite went up and up.
It went past the tops of the pines.
Then the wind stopped.
The kite fell to the grass.
Jane got it back up by tugging the string.
She smiled.
A pup ran past her.
The pup chased the kite.
Jane pulled it back up.
The pup hopped and jumped.
Jane and the pup went home glad.
It was such fun.

passages.imageAlt: A morning scene over a forest path between trees.

sentences.text: Tim and his pals had a big game.
They went to the lot at the back of the school.
Tim was on the red side.
His best pal Jake was on the white side.
The game was tag.
They had to chase and tag the rest.
Tim ran fast.
Jake ran fast too.
They had a long chase.
Tim got Jake!
Then Jake got Tim back.
They sat to rest.
The sun was hot.
They had a snack of ham and grapes.
The next game came.
Tim got the win.
Jake said: Fun game.
They went home with smiles.

passages.imageAlt: A midday scene over a country road winding through hills.

sentences.text: Dad and I had time to bake a cake.
We had ripe red plums in a pan.
Dad mixed up the cake mix.
Then I helped to slice the plums.
Dad added the plums to the mix.
He put the mix in a dish.
He set the dish in the stove.
We sat for a long time.
The cake smelled like home.
When it was set, dad took it from the stove.
The crust was tan and crisp.
We let the cake rest.
Then we ate slices and licked our lips.
The cake was the best!
What a fine cake!

passages.imageAlt: A morning scene over a warm sunlit window over a sill.

sentences.text: Last week, Mr. Carter took our class to the city park.
We rode in a yellow bus.
Mark sat next to me.
We sang songs and laughed all the way.
When we got there, our teacher gave us a map.
The park had a tall green tower, a pond, and a big wooden play set.
First, we went to the pond.
We saw a flock of ducks float by.
They quacked at us.
Next, we went to the play set.
I went down the long slide three times.
Mark went on the swing.
We had a snack of cheese, grapes, and milk.
Then we hiked to the top of the tower.
From up there, we could see the whole city.
The sun was bright in the sky.
We took photos and waved at trucks below.
The trip was the best one yet.
Our class had so much fun.

passages.imageAlt: A midday scene over a country road winding through hills.

sentences.text: On Sunday, Joy and her dad went to the beach.
They drove an hour in the car.
The sun was warm and the sky was clear blue.
Joy had a red beach pail and a small shovel.
She liked to dig in the sand.
Her dad set up an umbrella to give them shade.
Joy ran to the water and splashed her toes in the foam.
The water was cold but it felt great.
She found a smooth pink shell and showed it to her dad.
He smiled and said it was the best shell yet.
They had hot dogs and chips for lunch.
Then Joy and her dad made a big sandcastle.
It had three towers, a moat, and a flag on top.
A wave came up and washed it down.
Joy laughed.
They left at dusk with the sun glowing red in the west.

passages.imageAlt: A late afternoon scene over a gentle beach with waves.

sentences.text: There was an old barn at the back of the farm.
It had been there for a long time.
The walls were made of dark wood, and the roof was rusty red.
Grandpa said his dad had built the barn many years ago.
One Saturday, Grandpa and I went out to clean it.
We swept the dust, dragged out an old crate, and stacked the hay.
Inside, I found a brass key, an old horseshoe, and a black hat.
Grandpa said the hat was his when he was a kid.
He let me try it on.
Then we found a tiny brown mouse hiding in a corner.
It darted out the door so fast.
We laughed and kept on cleaning.
By lunchtime, the barn was tidy again.
Grandpa said I was a great helper.
He gave me the brass key as a gift.
I will keep it forever.

passages.imageAlt: A night scene over a forest path between trees.

sentences.text: The sky turned gray late on Friday.
Dad said a big storm was on the way.
We brought in the trash bin and the lawn chairs.
Mom shut all the windows tight.
My sister Beth and I helped pack a kit.
We put in flashlights, batteries, water, and snacks.
By that time, the wind started to howl.
Rain pounded down hard on the roof.
Then a flash of bright light split the sky and a loud crash of thunder rolled past.
Beth jumped and held my arm.
I told her it was just noise and she would be fine.
The lights blinked off.
We sat in the kitchen with two flashlights and a board game.
Dad made us hot cocoa on the gas stove.
Mom told us a funny story from when she was a kid.
By morning, the storm had passed.
The yard was wet, but we were safe.

passages.imageAlt: A dusk scene over a gentle beach with waves.

sentences.text: Last June, my dad and I went camping in the deep green woods.
We packed our tent, sleeping bags, fishing rods, a small grill, and lots of food.
The trip took us two hours by car and a short hike on a dirt path.
When we got to our spot, dad showed me how to set up the tent.
We pounded stakes into the ground and pulled the ropes tight.
Then we walked down to the creek.
I caught my very first trout.
Dad helped me clean it, and we cooked it over the fire for dinner.
After dinner, we sat by the fire and toasted big white marshmallows.
Dad told stories of when he was a boy and went camping with his dad.
I gazed up at the stars.
They were so bright, much brighter than back home.
I went to sleep happy and full of stories.

passages.imageAlt: A dusk scene over a gentle beach with waves.

sentences.text: For my eighth birthday, my mom and dad surprised me with a new pet.
It was a small gray and white kitten.
I named her Cloud because of her soft puffy fur.
She had bright green eyes and tiny pink toes.
The first day, Cloud was shy.
She hid under my bed for a long time.
I sat by the bed and called her with a soft voice.
Slowly she came out.
I set down a small dish of food and water.
After she ate, she let me pet her behind the ears.
By night, Cloud was curled up next to me on the rug.
The next morning, she ran around the house and chased a string.
She made me laugh so much.
Mom said Cloud was a fine cat and would be a great pal for me.
I love my new pet more than anything in the world.

passages.imageAlt: A late afternoon scene over a quiet playground in a field.
